name: Musicals
description: Broadway, West End, and beloved stage musicals

questions.q: The jukebox musical 'Mamma Mia!' is built around the hits of which Swedish pop group?

source.notes: First opened in London's West End in 1999.

questions.q: John Travolta played Danny Zuko, the leader of the T-Birds, in which 1978 movie musical?

source.notes: Opposite Olivia Newton-John as Sandy.

questions.q: The famous show tune 'Memory' comes from which Andrew Lloyd Webber musical?

source.notes: Sung by the cat Grizabella. Based on T.S. Eliot's poetry.

questions.q: What is the name of the scruffy dog in the musical 'Annie'?

source.notes: Annie and Sandy sing 'Tomorrow' together.

questions.q: The 2015 musical 'Hamilton' was created by which actor, composer, and lyricist?

source.notes: Won 11 Tony Awards in 2016.

questions.q: The ballad 'Don't Cry for Me Argentina' comes from which musical?

source.notes: Music by Andrew Lloyd Webber, lyrics by Tim Rice.

questions.q: The musical 'Grease' is set in which time period?

source.notes: Late 1950s — Rydell High, 1959.

questions.q: The musical 'The King and I' is set in the royal court of which country?

source.notes: Based on Anna Leonowens' memoirs; Rodgers and Hammerstein, 1951.

questions.q: The AIDS-era anthem 'Seasons of Love' is from which 1996 Tony-winning musical?

source.notes: Music and lyrics by Jonathan Larson; a modern update of 'La Bohème'.

questions.q: The musical 'Oliver!' is based on which Charles Dickens novel?

source.notes: 1960 musical by Lionel Bart.

questions.q: Which British composer wrote the music for 'Cats', 'The Phantom of the Opera', and 'Evita'?

source.notes: Made a life peer as Baron Lloyd-Webber of Sydmonton in 1997.

questions.q: The musical 'Wicked' is a prequel to what classic story?

source.notes: Based on Gregory Maguire's 1995 novel; tells the witches' backstory.

questions.q: The Disney animated film and stage musical 'The Lion King' features music by which British pop icon?

source.notes: With lyrics by Tim Rice; Zimmer scored the film itself.

questions.q: The 1981 musical 'Dreamgirls' is loosely based on the rise of which iconic Motown group?

source.notes: The 2006 film starred Beyoncé and won Jennifer Hudson the Best Supporting Actress Oscar.

questions.q: 'Defying Gravity' is a showstopping first-act song from which modern Broadway hit?

source.notes: Originally performed by Idina Menzel as Elphaba.

questions.q: The musical 'West Side Story' is a modern update of which Shakespeare play?

source.notes: 1957 musical with music by Leonard Bernstein; lyrics by Stephen Sondheim.

questions.q: The musical 'Chicago' features a famous opening number titled what?

source.notes: Written by Fred Ebb and John Kander; 2002 film won Best Picture.

questions.q: Which 1975 musical follows 17 Broadway dancers auditioning for a show?

source.notes: Won the Pulitzer Prize for Drama in 1976.

questions.q: 'Miss Saigon' is a modern update of which Puccini opera?

source.notes: By Claude-Michel Schönberg and Alain Boublil; first staged 1989.

questions.q: 'The Book of Mormon' was created by Trey Parker, Matt Stone, and which 'Avenue Q' composer?

source.notes: Opened March 2011; won 9 Tony Awards including Best Musical.

questions.q: 'The Phantom of the Opera' is based on a 1910 novel by which French writer?

source.notes: Set in the Paris Opera House.

questions.q: The 1957 musical 'The Music Man' is set in a small town in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Fictional 'River City, Iowa', 1912. Composed by Meredith Willson.
